Step 01
Know Who You Serve
The Old Me Exercise
Think about yourself before you became a coach. That person is your best client avatar.
The One Sentence — Write 3 versions. Circle the strongest.
get without
Your Path — Pick one and commit

Step 02 of 05
Step 02
Build Your Offer
The 4-Part Offer
Fill each part. One sentence each. Vague offers don’t sell.
Read This Out Loud. Fix What Sounds Awkward.

“I help you ________ in ________ through ________. If not, ________.”

Can’t say it without stumbling? Fix it today. An offer you can’t say out loud is one nobody will buy.

yogeshkaphle.com — Fitness Inside Out. Business Built In.

Step 03 of 05
Step 03
Build Trust
Last Week Audit — Be honest. This is just for you.

0 of 3 — no system. Start here.
1–2 of 3 — inconsistent. One missing piece is killing the momentum.
3 of 3 — on track. Now increase quality, not quantity.

This Week I Will Specifically Improve
My Strongest Proof Right Now

No client results yet? Your own transformation counts. Document the process. Show the work.

Step 04
Get In Front of Them
Path A or Path B — Pick one. Right now.

Both work. You can’t half-commit to both. Pick the one you can actually sustain right now.

If Path B — Commit the Numbers Now
Landing Page Status

Step 05
Sign & Deliver
Consultation — 3 Questions You Ask Every Prospect First
You’re not pitching. You’re diagnosing. Write these before your next call.
The 3 Cs Audit — How you deliver after they sign

Missing all three? Start with Curriculum. It’s the foundation everything else sits on.

The First C I Will Build This Month

Referrals don’t come from better marketing. They come from results that surprised someone. Deliver those.
